title: Labour Market Impact Assessment Online Portal resources - Canada.ca
content:
## LMIA Online resources
- What you need to do before you apply using LMIA Online
- What you can do in the LMIA Online portal
- Uploading documents in LMIA Online
- Videos to help you use LMIA Online
- LMIA Online: Troubleshooting
LMIA Online is a reliable and secure online portal. It allows Canadian employers and third-party representatives (on behalf of employers) to submit Labour Market Impact Assessment (LMIA) applications to Service Canada electronically.

## What you need to do before you apply using  LMIA Online
- Canadian employers need to have a payroll account number associated with a business number issued by the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA)
- To sign in to LMIA Online, you must first create your own user account on Job Bank . Before you can submit an LMIA application, you also have to register the business on Job Bank . Visit Job Bank’s page for employers to learn more
Important information for third-party representatives
If you’re a third-party representative and you want to apply for an LMIA on behalf of an employer, both you and the employer (such as the owner or an employer contact) must have a personal Job Bank account. You must also both be added to the “Users” section of the employer file for the business. The person who manages the employer file can easily add new users to the file on their own without Job Bank support.
- Review the program’s eligibility requirements for the stream you’re applying for: Agricultural Global Talent Stream (including your Labour Market Benefits Plan) High wage Foreign academic Low wage In-home caregiver Permanent residency Seasonal Agricultural Worker Program
- Global Talent Stream (including your Labour Market Benefits Plan)
- High wage Foreign academic
- Low wage In-home caregiver
- Seasonal Agricultural Worker Program
- Gather documents that support your business legitimacy and your LMIA application. For example: lease agreement tax documents recruitment documents (for example: ads)
- recruitment documents (for example: ads)
- For positions located in Quebec for an employment period of more than 30 consecutive days, visit Hiring in the province of Quebec
- Log in to LMIA Online to apply now

## What you can do in LMIA Online
Once you create a Job Bank account, you'll be able to do the following in LMIA Online:
- submit an LMIA application 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
- complete or return to an LMIA application before submitting it to Service Canada
- upload supporting documents
- securely pay LMIA fees, if applicable
- get real-time updates on the status of your application
- view correspondence and decision letters issued by Service Canada
- review previously submitted applications
- access and follow the status of your application when a third-party representative is submitting an LMIA application on your behalf
- create a new LMIA application by copying the information from a previous application

## Uploading documents in LMIA Online
The size limit of each document is 6.0 MB . If you need to upload a larger document, divide your file into multiple documents smaller than 6.0 MB each.
- Documents must be in PDF, RTF, TXT or JPG format
- There's no limit on the number of documents that you can upload in LMIA Online
Leave out confidential information
Don’t upload any documents containing confidential information, such as:
- social insurance number (SIN)

## Videos to help you use LMIA Online
The following videos guide you on how to use LMIA Online and what's in it for you!
Introduction to the LMIA Online portal
Logging into the LMIA Online portal
Starting the LMIA application in the LMIA Online portal
Finishing the LMIA application in the LMIA Online portal
Making a payment in the LMIA Online portal
Viewing the decision letter in the LMIA Online portal

## LMIA Online: Troubleshooting
Some employers may be unable to access LMIA Online because they can’t create a Job Bank account or they need technical support.
If this applies to you, read the relevant sections below for more information.

### If you can’t create a Job Bank account
To use LMIA Online, you need to create a Job Bank account using:
- a payroll account number associated with a business number issued by the CRA
If you’re unable to create an account, you can still submit an LMIA application by requesting the LMIA application form .

### If you need technical support
Contact the relevant team to get help:
- LMIA Online support: ptet.eimtenligne-lmiaonline.tfwp@servicecanada.gc.ca
If your issue persists, you can request the LMIA application form to submit an application.

### How to request the LMIA application form
If your technical issues persist or you can’t get a Job Bank account, contact the LMIA forms team by email for a copy of the LMIA application form: EDSC.DEMANDE.FORMULAIRE.PTET-TFWP.FORM.REQUEST.ESDC@servicecanada.gc.ca
If you’re a third-party representative, you can submit this request on behalf of your client.
Include the following information in your email:
- steps you’ve taken to resolve the issues preventing you from using LMIA Online
- streams you’d like to apply to
We’ll review your request to determine whether an application form is justified in your case. You’ll get a reply by email within 5 business days.

### How to submit an LMIA application form
If your request is justified, we'll send you an email with the LMIA application forms along with instructions on how to submit your application to the appropriate processing centre .
Application forms get updated regularly. Make sure you’re submitting the most recent version number, which can be found in the lower left corner of the form.
Seasonal Agricultural Worker Program
Global Talent Stream
Permanent resident stream
- ptet.eimtenligne-lmiaonline.tfwp@servicecanada.gc.ca

### Information on the Program and your LMIA application
- Employer Contact Centre

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
